svg.text: 5.18 · Indexing the ledger — every index justified by an access pattern
fast reads · cheap writes · enforced integrity · nothing redundant — as it grows to billions of rows
ledger_entry (the big, growing table)
CLUSTERED PK (account_id, created_at, txn_id, line_no)
→ statement locality + range + ORDER BY, no filesort (5.4/5.7/5.10)
+ covering column: amount
→ hot balance/statement reads = "Using index", no row fetch (5.6)
secondary (transaction_id) — only if "all legs" is hot
→ added ONLY for a real access pattern (5.16)
✗ dropped: standalone (account_id) — redundant via leftmost-prefix
transaction_
PK (transaction_id) BIGINT UNSIGNED
compact, monotonic → append-only inserts (5.15)
UNIQUE (idempotency_key) — integrity + lookup (5.17, M16)
account
PK (account_id) · status via FK lookup table (M03/3.10)
why this set scales in reads, writes & correctness together
● compact monotonic/time-ordered keys (M03/3.12) → small indexes, append-only inserts, no page splits (5.15)
● clustered PK shaped for the dominant query (M01/1.14) → statements are sequential leaf walks
● covering only the HOT reads → fast reads without over-widening every index (5.6/5.16)
● UNIQUE idempotency key → double-posting structurally impossible (5.17) · ✗ no redundant indexes (5.16)
